display.purpose: Hero H1 only. The single loudest element; capped at 64px.

title.purpose: Card titles, feature names, nav brand.

body.purpose: Default prose; ledes at 17px Ink 500, capped ~56ch.

label.purpose: Uppercase tracked kicker. Rationed, not on every section.

mono.purpose: API-key chips and code-like tokens only.

shadows.name: ambient-z1
shadows.purpose: Barely-there resting lift for cards and sliders.

shadows.name: ambient-z2
shadows.purpose: Raised state for popovers, active demo cards, hover lift.

shadows.name: floating-glass-nav
shadows.purpose: The sticky frosted nav pill — the only sanctioned glass element.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing (--ease) for every state transition.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The default CTA everywhere — ink-black fill, quiet and confident.

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Lower-commitment CTA — white fill, hairline border that darkens on hover.

components.name: Live Status Chip
components.description: Signature component — marks a shipping-today module. Carries the color budget.

components.name: Coming-Soon Chip
components.description: Teal roadmap marker for integrations and workflows not yet live.

components.name: Card
components.description: Resting surface — flat, white, hairline-bordered. Depth is a state response, not a resting property.
components.html:
Sales Invoicing

Create, send, and track invoices. Stay on top of what's owed and what's paid.

components.name: Floating Nav Pill
components.description: The one glass element — a frosted pill that firms up on scroll.
components.html: KomKitsProductPricing

narrative.overview: KomKits presents the whole of a business — invoicing, inventory, payments, returns — on a single monochrome instrument surface where everything is visible at once and nothing shouts. Ink (#111214) on white (#FFFFFF), with a small grey scale for every intermediate tone. Color is not decoration; it is a signal light — the only saturated hues are the three status colors (live-green, coming-soon-teal, attention-amber), used exclusively to mark state. This is capable, calm, modern made visual, aimed at effortless relief. It rejects the dark dev-tool aesthetic, maximalist Framer motion, playful Clay 3D, dense legacy-ERP chrome, and generic AI-SaaS slop.
keyCharacteristics: Ink-on-white monochrome; a single near-black plus a four-step grey scale.
Status color is the only saturation — green (live), teal (coming-soon), amber (attention).
Flat surfaces, hairline #E4E4E7 borders, soft ambient shadows; frosted glass reserved for the floating nav.
One typeface, Public Sans, worked across weights 400–800.
Tight display tracking (-0.035em), generous 88px section rhythm.
Motion is scrubbed and reduced-motion-safe; content renders without JS.

rules.name: The Signal-Light Rule
rules.body: Saturated color is forbidden as decoration. Green, teal, and amber appear only to communicate status — live, coming-soon, attention. If a color isn't reporting state, it must be ink or grey.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: There is exactly one brand color and it is near-black. Do not introduce a secondary accent to warm up the page; warmth is carried by copy, spacing, and real product UI.

rules.name: The Rationed Eyebrow Rule
rules.body: The uppercase tracked eyebrow label exists, but a kicker above every section is the AI-slop tell. Use it only where a section genuinely needs a category tag.

rules.name: The One-Family Rule
rules.body: Public Sans is the only typeface. Contrast comes from weight (400 vs 800) and tracking, not from a new family.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest and bounded by a 1px #E4E4E7 border. Shadow is a response to state (hover, raise, float), not a resting property of every card.

rules.name: The One Glass Rule
rules.body: Backdrop-blur belongs to the nav and nowhere else. It is a signal that an element floats above the document.

dos: Do keep the body ink-on-white: #111214 on #FFFFFF, with the Grey 50/100/200 scale for every intermediate surface.
Do reserve all saturation for status — green #00A04D (live), teal #008694 (coming-soon), amber #AD6800 (attention) — via the chip system.
Do bound surfaces with 1px #E4E4E7 borders and keep them flat; add Ambient z1/z2 shadow only as a state response.
Do carry hierarchy with Public Sans weight and tracking alone (400 body vs 800 headline; -0.035em display).
Do cap the hero display at 64px, ledes at ~56ch, and hold to the 88px section rhythm (64px on mobile).
Do give every animation a prefers-reduced-motion path and render content without JS.
donts: Don't ship the dark dev-tool aesthetic — no dark-mode-default, no neon accent. This is light and business-facing, not Vercel/Cursor.
Don't add maximalist Framer-style motion — one earned motion moment per section, scrubbed not scheduled.
Don't introduce playful Clay-style 3D illustration or pastel blobs; this is a tool people trust with money.
Don't regress to the legacy accounting/ERP look — no dense gray spreadsheet chrome.
Don't produce generic AI-SaaS slop: no cream/parchment body, no tracked uppercase eyebrow above every section, no endless identical icon-card grids.
Don't use color as decoration. If a hue isn't reporting status, make it ink or grey.
Don't introduce a second typeface or a second brand accent. One family, one voice.
Don't apply glassmorphism anywhere but the nav.
Don't let muted text fall below Ink 400 (#9CA3AF) on white, or below 4.5:1 for body copy.
